info.name: NYCTaxiData API

variable.description: Base URL of the NYCTaxiData API. Change this to match your environment.
JWT Bearer token obtained from POST /api/v1/auth/login or register. Set this after login.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints — login, registration, OTP, password reset, token refresh, and profile retrieval. Route prefix: /api/v1/auth

request.description:
Authenticates a user (driver or manager) with phone number and password. Returns a JWT token on success.

Command: LoginCommand(PhoneNumber, Password)

Registers a new driver account.

Command: RegisterDriverCommand(FirstName, LastName, PhoneNumber, Password, Age, City, Street, LicenseNumber, PlateNumber)

Registers a new manager account.

Command: RegisterManagerCommand(FirstName, LastName, PhoneNumber, Password, Age, City, Street, EmployeeId, Department)

Sends a one-time password (OTP) to the specified phone number via WhatsApp/SMS. Used for phone verification or password reset flow.

Command: SendOtpCommand(PhoneNumber)

Verifies a previously sent OTP code. Returns a reset token on success.

Command: VerifyOtpCommand(PhoneNumber, OtpCode)

Resets the user's password using a valid reset token obtained from the OTP verification step.

Command: ResetPasswordCommand(ResetToken, NewPassword)

Refreshes an expired JWT token by providing the old token. Returns a new token on success.

Command: RefreshTokenCommand(OldToken)

variable.description: The phone number of the user whose profile to retrieve.

request.description:
Retrieves the profile of a user by their phone number.

Query: GetProfileQuery(phoneNumber)
Route param: phoneNumber

item.name: Trips
item.description: Trip management endpoints — CRUD operations, lifecycle actions (start/end), dispatch, and rich analytics. Route prefix: /api/v1/trips

query.description: Filter trips from this date (optional, ISO 8601)
Filter trips until this date (optional, ISO 8601)

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of trips with optional filters.

Query: GetAllTripsQuery(PageNumber, PageSize, StartDate, EndDate, DriverId, ProcessStatus)

Retrieves a single trip by its integer ID.

Query: GetTripByIdQuery(TripId)

Returns paginated trips filtered by a specific taxi zone.

Query: GetTripsByZoneQuery(ZoneId, PageNumber, PageSize)

Creates a new trip record.

Command: CreateTripCommand(DriverId, PickupLocationId, DropoffLocationId, FareAmount, TipAmount)

variable.description: Must match tripId in the request body

request.description:
Updates an existing trip's fare, tip, and processing status. The ID in the URL must match the TripId in the body.

Command: UpdateTripCommand(TripId, FareAmount, TipAmount, ProcessStatus)

Permanently deletes a trip by its ID.

Command: DeleteTripCommand(TripId)

Marks a trip as started (In-Progress). Updates driver status and logs the start time.

Command: StartTripCommand(TripId, DriverId, PickupLocationId, DropoffLocationId)

Marks a trip as completed. Calculates the final fare based on duration and pricing parameters.

Command: EndTripCommand(TripId, FarePerMinute=0.5, BaseFare=2.50, SurgeMultiplier=1.0)

Returns paginated trip history, optionally filtered by driver.

Query: GetTripHistoryQuery(DriverId, PageNumber, PageSize)
DEV/TEST endpoint — Creates a minimal trip to verify audit logging (CreatedBy, CreatedAt) is functioning correctly.

Returns a paginated list of currently online/available drivers.

Spec: AvailableDriversSpec(page, limit)

query.description: Max number of dispatch items to return (default: 20)
Time window in minutes to look back (default: 60)

request.description:
Returns a live feed of recent dispatch events within a rolling time window.

Query: GetLiveDispatchFeedQuery(Limit, MinutesWindow)

Manually dispatches a driver to a passenger. Supports optional trip linking and smart routing.

Command: ManualDispatchCommand(DriverId, PickupZoneId, DropoffZoneId, PassengerName, PassengerPhone, Priority, SmartRoutingEnabled, TripId)

Updates driver availability status and current GPS location.

Command: UpdateDriverStatusCommand(DriverId, Status, CurrentLat, CurrentLng)

Returns aggregate trip statistics (total trips, avg fare, etc.).

Query: GetTripsStatisticsQuery()

Returns revenue statistics within an optional date range.

Query: GetRevenueStatisticsQuery(StartDate, EndDate)

Returns demand statistics within an optional date range.

Query: GetDemandStatisticsQuery(StartDate, EndDate)

Returns trip trend data over time.

Query: GetTripTrendsQuery()

Returns driver activity statistics.

Query: GetDriverActivityQuery()

item.name: Drivers
item.description: Driver management endpoints — list, profile, fleet status, shift stats, status updates, and offline data sync. Route prefix: /api/v1/drivers

query.description: Filter by driver status (e.g. Available, Busy, Offline)

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of drivers with optional status and zone filters.

Query: GetDriverListQuery(Status, ZoneId, PageNumber, PageSize)

Returns a paginated list of active fleet drivers (excludes Offline status).

Query: GetActiveFleetQuery(PageNumber, PageSize)

Returns detailed profile and current statistics for a specific driver.

Query: GetDriverProfileQuery(DriverId)

Returns shift statistics for a driver within an optional time window.

Query: GetShiftStatisticsQuery(DriverId, ShiftStartUtc, ShiftEndUtc)

Updates driver availability status and current GPS coordinates.

Request body: UpdateDriverStatusRequest(Status, CurrentLat, CurrentLng)
Command: UpdateDriverStatusCommand(DriverId, Status, CurrentLat, CurrentLng)

Synchronizes batched offline trip data collected when the driver had no network connectivity.

Command: SyncOfflineDataCommand(DriverId, Trips[OfflineTripDto(LocalTripId, PickupLocationId, DropoffLocationId, StartedAt, EndedAt, ActualFare)])

item.name: Zones
item.description: Zone analytics and intelligence endpoints — metadata, statistics, heatmaps, trends, comparisons, and driver distribution. Route prefix: /api/v1/zones

request.description:
Returns all NYC taxi zones.

Query: GetAllZonesQuery()

Returns metadata for all zones (names, borough, shape data).

Query: GetZoneMetadataQuery()

Retrieves details of a single zone by its integer ID.

Query: GetZoneByIdQuery(id)

Returns AI-generated insights for a specific zone.

Query: GetZoneInsightsQuery(id)

query.description: Zone IDs to compare (send multiple times for each ID)
Number of recommended zones to return (default: 10)
Zone ID to get trends for (optional — all zones if omitted)

request.description:
Returns trend data for a zone or all zones.

Query: GetZoneTrendsQuery(zoneId, trendType)

Returns historical zone data within a date range.

Query: GetZoneHistoryQuery(zoneId, startDate, endDate)

query.description: Zone ID (optional — all zones if omitted)

request.description:
Returns the current distribution of drivers across all zones.

Query: GetDriverDistributionQuery()

query.description: Number of top zones to return (default: 10)
Number of top zones to return (default: 10)
Number of high-risk zones to return (default: 10)

item.name: Analytics
item.description: Strategic analytics dashboard endpoints — KPIs, demand velocity, and system threshold management. Requires Admin or Dispatcher role. Route prefix: /api/v1/analytics

request.description:
Returns the top-level KPIs for the dashboard (active drivers, total revenue, average wait time, etc.).

Requires: Admin or Dispatcher role
Query: GetTopLevelKpisQuery()

query.description: Optional zone ID filter. Omit for all zones.
Number of hours to look back (default: 24)

request.description:
Returns time-series chart data for AI-predicted demand velocity.

Requires: Admin or Dispatcher role
Query: GetDemandVelocityChartQuery(ZoneId, Hours)

Returns the current system configuration for surge price multipliers and dispatch search radius.

Requires: Admin or Dispatcher role
Query: GetSystemThresholdsQuery()

Updates system-wide thresholds for surge pricing and dispatch search radius.

Requires: Admin or Dispatcher role
Command: UpdateSystemThresholdsCommand(SurgeMultipliers, DispatchRadiusKm)

item.name: AI Predictions
item.description: Machine learning inference endpoints for demand forecasting, ETA prediction, revenue prediction, stockout risk, and vehicle repositioning optimization. Route prefix: /api/v1/ai

request.description:
Predicts 15-minute trip demand for a list of zones at a specified target time.

Query: GetDemandForecast15MinQuery(ZoneIds, TargetTime, RoundToInt)

Predicts 6-hour demand forecast for a list of zones.

Query: GetDemandForecast6hQuery(ZoneIds, TargetTime)

Predicts ETA for one or more routes (zone pairs).

Query: GetEtaPredictionQuery(Routes: List<RouteRequest(PickupZoneId, DropoffZoneId, TargetTime)>)

Predicts revenue for a list of zones at a target time.

Query: GetRevenuePredictionQuery(ZoneIds, TargetTime)

Predicts driver stockout (supply shortage) probability for a list of zones.

Query: GetStockOutPredictionQuery(ZoneIds, TargetTime)

Generates an optimized fleet repositioning plan based on current supply/demand state across zones.

Command: OptimizeRepositioningCommand(TimeWindow, ZoneStates: List<ZoneSupplyState>, Constraints: OptimizationConstraints)

Optimizes driver distribution across zones to maximize profit in the next 6 hours.

Command: OptimizeProfitMaximizationCommand(ZoneStates: ProfitMaximizationInput[])
Returns: ApiResponse<ProfitMaximizationResult>

item.name: Simulation
item.description: Real-time taxi fleet simulation engine — control the simulation lifecycle (start/pause/resume/stop), adjust speed, query status, and retrieve playback data. Route prefix: /api/v1/simulation

request.description:
Starts a new faster-than-real-time simulation run.

Body: SimulationStartRequest(DurationHours[1-720], SpeedFactor[1-200], TotalDrivers[1-10000], ZoneCount[1-265], StartTime)
Pauses the currently running simulation.
Resumes a paused simulation.
Stops and terminates the current simulation session.

Adjusts the speed multiplier of the running simulation.

Body: SimulationSpeedRequest(SpeedFactor: double)
Returns the current status snapshot of the simulation (ID, status, simulated time, speed factor).
Returns a playback chunk for scrubbing through recorded simulation ticks.
Returns the list of zone IDs included in the current simulation run.
Returns the full metric history for a specific zone during the current simulation.
Returns and compares simulation metric history for two zones side by side.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Administrative endpoints for data aggregation and daily statistics management. Requires Manager role. Route prefix: /api/v1/admin

request.description:
Triggers daily aggregation for today's date. Computes and stores DailyStats from raw trip data.

Requires: Manager role

variable.description: Target date to aggregate (cannot be a future date). Format: yyyy-MM-dd

request.description:
Triggers aggregation for a specific historical date. Returns 400 if a future date is provided.

Requires: Manager role

query.description: Start date for stats range (optional, default: 30 days ago). Format: yyyy-MM-dd
End date for stats range (optional, default: today). Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Max range: 365 days.

request.description:
Retrieves pre-aggregated DailyStats records within a date range. Returns a max of 365 days.

Requires: Manager role

item.name: SignalR Hubs (WebSocket)
item.description:
Real-time WebSocket hubs using ASP.NET Core SignalR. Connect using a SignalR client library. JWT token can be passed via query param: ?access_token={{authToken}}

Hub endpoints (not standard HTTP — use SignalR client):
• /hubs/taxi — General taxi status hub
• /hubs/tracking — Live GPS tracking hub
• /hubs/dispatch — Real-time dispatch notification hub
• /hubs/simulation — Simulation event streaming hub

query.description: JWT token for SignalR authentication (passed as query param)

request.description:
SignalR Hub: TaxiHub
Path: /hubs/taxi

Use a SignalR client (not a plain HTTP request) to connect.
JWT is passed via ?access_token= query string for WebSocket negotiation.

This request is for documentation purposes only.

SignalR Hub: LiveTrackingHub
Path: /hubs/tracking

Streams real-time GPS locations for active drivers.
Use a SignalR client to subscribe to tracking events.

SignalR Hub: DispatchHub
Path: /hubs/dispatch

Sends real-time dispatch notifications to drivers and dispatchers.
Use a SignalR client to receive dispatch events.

SignalR Hub: SimulationHub
Path: /hubs/simulation

Streams real-time SimulationTick events during an active simulation run.
Each tick contains aggregate metrics and per-zone snapshots.
